title: MTU constants not enforced at compile time
description:
lichen_l2.h:49 defines LICHEN_L2_MTU = 200 with comment 'Must match LICHEN_LORA_MTU in lora_l2.h'. lora_l2.h:57 computes LICHEN_LORA_MTU = 255 - 55 = 200. No compile-time assertion links them.

What goes wrong: If either constant changes independently (e.g., frame overhead increases), one layer rejects packets the other accepts. Silent packet loss or -EMSGSIZE errors with no obvious root cause.

Fix: Add BUILD_ASSERT(LICHEN_L2_MTU == LICHEN_LORA_MTU) in lichen_l2.c after including both headers.
close_reason: Fixed: Added BUILD_ASSERT(LICHEN_L2_MTU == LICHEN_LORA_MTU) in lichen_l2.c after including both headers. This ensures compile-time failure if either MTU constant changes independently.
